actions.title: Relieves wheezing and dyspnea, stops cough

branches.pattern: Wind-cold constriction of the Lung leading to Lung qi reversal
branches.sub_pattern: Cough from wind-cold attacking the exterior and constricting Lung qi
Wheezing or cough caused by Lung heat

branches.pattern: Wind-cold attack at the exterior with cold stagnation in the interior
branches.sub_pattern: Wheezing or cough from wind-cold attack with interior cold stagnation
Wheezing and dyspnea caused by stagnation of qi and phlegm

actions.title: Regulates water circulation and relieves edema

branches.pattern: Arteritis obliterans / obliterating phlebitis / Raynaud’s disease

functions: Strongly releases the exterior and disperses wind-cold
Promotes sweating in exterior excess wind-cold patterns
dui_yao.notes: Classic pair for exterior excess wind-cold without sweating; strongly opens the pores and releases the pathogen. Representative formula: Ma Huang Tang.

functions: One disperses while the other descends
dui_yao.notes: A fundamental pair for cough, wheezing, and dyspnea. Commonly used for wind-cold constraining the Lung or other patterns with impaired Lung qi diffusion and descent.

functions: Clears Lung heat while relieving wheezing
Simultaneously vents constrained exterior qi and drains internal heat
dui_yao.notes: Used when wheezing is due to Lung heat. Shi Gao moderates the warm, dispersing nature of Ma Huang. Representative formula: Ma Xing Gan Shi Tang.

functions: Warms the Lung and transforms thin mucus
Disperses cold from exterior and interior
dui_yao.notes: Useful in cold-type wheezing or cough with thin, profuse phlegm and internal cold. Often seen in Xiao Qing Long Tang-type presentations.
A strong cold-dispersing pair for exterior cold with internal cold-fluid retention. Often combined with Gan Jiang and Ban Xia.
Used when phlegm retention contributes to dyspnea or cough, especially in cold-phlegm or cold-fluid patterns.

functions: Releases the exterior and promotes urination
Reduces edema associated with exterior wind-water patterns
dui_yao.notes: Useful for edema with an exterior syndrome. Bai Zhu strengthens the Spleen and assists fluid metabolism. Representative formula: Yue Bi Jia Zhu Tang.

functions: Warms and harmonizes the Lung and Stomach
Supports the dispersing action of Ma Huang
dui_yao.notes: A supportive pair often used in formulas for wind-cold or wind-water patterns.
Used in wind-damp painful obstruction when exterior cold-damp and channel obstruction are prominent.

functions: Opens obstruction in yin-type sores or cold stagnation disorders
dui_yao.notes: A stronger warming and dispersing combination often seen in chronic cold-type obstruction or yin sores.
